meta.description: Run Gene Ontology (GO) and KEGG pathway enrichment analysis on gene lists using clusterProfiler. Inputs: DEG or candidate gene list, organism background. Outputs: enrichment result tables, dot plots, bar plots, enrichment map.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, citations, or invented biological claims were observed in any tested output.

practice_boundaries.detail: Outputs stayed within enrichment-analysis scope and did not provide diagnostic or prescriptive medical advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The ORA-based GO and KEGG workflow was appropriate for the tested gene-list inputs, although one summary line overstated empty KEGG results.

code_usability.detail: R scripts executed successfully on four valid scenarios and failed safely with a controlled validation error on the invalid scenario.

functional_suitability.note: Core enrichment, plotting, and reporting coverage is strong after the empty-result summary fix, with only minor recovery guidance gaps remaining.

reliability.note: Validation and regression coverage are solid, and empty-result handling now aligns warnings with final summary states.

performance_context.note: Documentation is layered reasonably well and the workflow is concise, though the SKILL.md still carries a fair amount of inline detail.

agent_usability.note: Sectioning and expected outputs are clear, and the repaired CLI guide now provides consistent examples for agents to follow.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural and the fixed CLI examples improve forgiveness for users following the documentation literally.

security.note: No unsafe shell execution or credential handling issues were found, and user parameters are validated before analysis starts.

maintainability.note: The code is modular and testable, but duplicated documentation content increases drift risk across references and behavior.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision and scope boundaries are good, and partial-success reporting is now clearer for empty enrichment outputs.

inputs.label: Human SYMBOL smoke test
inputs.note: Executed perfectly and produced all documented outputs.

assertions.text: Output completes GO, KEGG, plotting, and session-info generation
assertions.note: All expected artifacts were generated successfully.

assertions.text: Output reports parsed gene count and final status fields
assertions.note: Summary lines include parsed count plus GO, KEGG, and plot status.

assertions.text: Output enumerates key files promised by the skill
assertions.note: The summary lists CSV, RDA, plot, and session-info files.

assertions.text: Output stays within enrichment-analysis scope
assertions.note: No unsupported analysis or fabricated interpretation was added.

inputs.label: Mouse ENSEMBL example
inputs.note: Completed cleanly with a KEGG-empty warning and an accurate EMPTY summary state.

assertions.text: Output handles the documented mouse ENSEMBL input without crashing
assertions.note: The command finished and produced GO and plot artifacts.

assertions.text: Output warns when KEGG returns no enriched pathways
assertions.note: A SKILL_EMPTY_DATA warning was emitted for KEGG.

assertions.text: Summary does not overstate KEGG success when KEGG results are empty
assertions.note: The rerun reported summary.kegg_status as EMPTY for a header-only KEGG CSV.

assertions.text: Plot generation degrades gracefully when only GO results remain
assertions.note: The dot-chart data file contains GO rows and the plot was still created.

assertions.text: Run still records reproducibility metadata
assertions.note: session_info.txt was written successfully.

inputs.label: Unsupported species validation
inputs.note: Failed safely with a clear SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER error before analysis work began.

assertions.text: Unsupported species is rejected safely before downstream analysis
assertions.note: The run stopped on validation with no partial outputs reported.

assertions.text: Error output includes an exact SKILL code
assertions.note: The emitted code was SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER.

assertions.text: Error output gives a concrete next step for retry
assertions.note: The message names the bad species but does not list supported alternatives or a retry hint.

assertions.text: Failure path avoids misleading success summaries
assertions.note: No success summary was emitted after the validation failure.

inputs.label: Mixed-separator human input
inputs.note: Executed cleanly and confirmed separator normalization in the logs.

assertions.text: Mixed separators are normalized into the expected four genes
assertions.note: The parsed feature line showed TP53, EGFR, BRCA1, and MYC.

assertions.text: The documented SVG output path is produced
assertions.note: The run wrote gokegg_dot_chart.svg successfully.

assertions.text: Summary reports the expected artifact set
assertions.note: The output directory and key files were listed in the summary block.

assertions.text: Output remains within the skill's stated scope
assertions.note: Only enrichment and visualization behavior was performed.

inputs.label: Custom plotting parameter run
inputs.note: Handled the multi-parameter plotting request cleanly and produced all expected artifacts.

assertions.text: Advanced plotting parameters execute successfully
assertions.note: PNG output was generated with the requested custom settings.

assertions.text: Output keeps the documented summary structure
assertions.note: Parsed count, statuses, output directory, and key files were all present.

assertions.text: Stress input does not break reproducibility controls
assertions.note: The run honored the fixed seed and completed without instability.

assertions.text: Output does not drift outside the skill scope under multi-parameter input
assertions.note: No unrelated analysis or unsafe operation appeared.

assertions.text: Plot data artifacts are preserved for downstream reuse
assertions.note: Both CSV and RDA plot bundles were written successfully.

key_strengths: The core R workflow is genuinely executable and passed four non-trivial runtime scenarios in this audit.
Input validation and regression tests cover important boundary cases such as separator parsing, empty input, and malformed plotting parameters.
Documentation clearly states supported species, gene ID types, expected artifacts, and out-of-scope analysis types, and the CLI guide examples are now complete and consistent.
The implementation is modular, separating parsing, analysis, plotting, and utility logic into focused script files.

recommendations.title: Strengthen failure-path recovery hints
recommendations.problem: Validation failures surface the right SKILL code, but some messages stop short of giving the user an immediate retry path.
recommendations.root_cause: Error messages focus on the failing value and not on the supported alternatives or next-step examples.
recommendations.fix: Expand high-frequency validation errors to include the accepted choices directly in the message, for example the supported species list or a short retry template.
